description:
You are an edge-computing engineer preparing a software rollout to a fleet of IoT thermostats.  
    Before deploying, you must generate a very small report indicating which devices are currently **offline** so that the update can be postponed for them.

    Existing material on disk:
      • /home/user/iot/deployment/device_status.json  
        ‑ JSON document containing an array of objects, one object per thermostat.  
      • /home/user/iot/deployment/device_status_schema.json  
        ‑ Draft-07 JSON-Schema describing the expected shape of every array element.  
        (The schema is already correct and is supplied only for reference; you do NOT need to edit it.)

    Task to perform (single terminal command is enough, pipelines allowed):
      1. Use jq to read /home/user/iot/deployment/device_status.json.  
      2. Select every element whose "status" property is the literal string "offline".  
      3. From those elements collect only their "device_id" property values.  
      4. Assemble the result as a compact JSON array (no spaces or line breaks except the single newline automatically added by the shell redirect, if any).  
         Example format: ["dev-a12","dev-b34"]  
      5. Write that exact array to:
            /home/user/iot/deployment/offline_devices.json
         The file must be overwritten if it already exists.

      6. In the same command, append a one-line log entry to:
            /home/user/iot/logs/offline_devices.log
         The line must follow precisely this template (no surrounding quotes):
            OFFLINE_DEVICES: <N>
         where <N> is the count of elements you just wrote to offline_devices.json.  
         Do NOT add any other text or extra blank lines.

    Formatting requirements that the automated test will check:
      • /home/user/iot/deployment/offline_devices.json must consist of **exactly one line** that is valid JSON.  
      • /home/user/iot/logs/offline_devices.log must have the new line appended; its last line must match the regex:
            ^OFFLINE_DEVICES: [0-9]+$
      • The numerical value in the log line must equal the number of array elements written to offline_devices.json.

    Hints (do NOT echo these as part of your answer):
      • `jq -c` produces compact output.  
      • You can chain commands with `&&` or `;` so everything happens in one shell line.

    Return control to the terminal when you are finished; no additional commentary is required.
truth:
Initial files placed before the agent starts:

    1. /home/user/iot/deployment/device_status.json
       Contents (single JSON array, pretty-printed for readability here):
       [
         {
           "device_id": "edge-therm-01",
           "status": "online",
           "ip": "192.168.10.21"
         },
         {
           "device_id": "edge-therm-02",
           "status": "offline",
           "ip": "192.168.10.22"
         },
         {
           "device_id": "edge-therm-03",
           "status": "online",
           "ip": "192.168.10.23"
         },
         {
           "device_id": "edge-therm-04",
           "status": "maintenance",
           "ip": "192.168.10.24"
         },
         {
           "device_id": "edge-therm-05",
           "status": "offline",
           "ip": "192.168.10.25"
         }
       ]

    2. /home/user/iot/deployment/device_status_schema.json  
       (valid Draft-07 schema; its exact content is irrelevant for verification).

    3. /home/user/iot/logs/ directory exists and is writable by the regular user; the
       file offline_devices.log may or may not exist yet.

    Expected artefacts after correct task completion:

    A. /home/user/iot/deployment/offline_devices.json
       Exact content (one line, newline at EOF is acceptable):
       ["edge-therm-02","edge-therm-05"]

    B. /home/user/iot/logs/offline_devices.log
       Its **last** line must be exactly:
       OFFLINE_DEVICES: 2
       (If the file existed beforehand, earlier lines should be preserved.)

    The automated tests will:
       • Check that offline_devices.json exists, is non-empty, contains valid JSON, and matches the exact array above.  
       • Read the final line of offline_devices.log and compare it to the string "OFFLINE_DEVICES: 2".  
       • Ensure that the count in the log equals the length of the JSON array.
